reviewScope: Re-entry review. Page previously passed all three reviews. Single targeted change on line 158 (Pages section) — added ancestor permission requirement and updated cross-reference anchor.

invariantResults.evidence: 4-space indentation used consistently throughout. No tabs or 2-space indents found. Modified line 158 maintains correct indentation at column 0 (paragraph text).
Filename 'content-sync.md' uses lowercase hyphenated format matching the page title 'Content sync'.
Page title 'Content sync' does not end with '-ing'.
Three numbered lists found: line 143 (6 steps), line 160 (7 steps), line 207 (4 steps). All within the 8-step maximum.
Pronouns have clear antecedents throughout. Modified line uses 'you' (reader) and 'its' (referring to 'the page') with clear antecedents.
Application references use correct phrasing: 'Open the Content hub application' (line 143), 'Open a website channel application' (line 160), 'Open the Forms application' (line 207).
No instances of 'braces' found. Page does not reference curly brackets.
Unicode rightwards arrow '→' used in navigation paths (line 81: 'After form submission → Redirect to').

UI element names are bolded: **Content hub** (line 143), **Sync** (lines 146, 152, 171, 208, 211), **Include forms** (line 66), **Development**, **Staging**, **Production** (line 19), **Delete** (line 239), **Draft (Initial)** (line 93), etc.

Italics used correctly for object/feature names: *Read* and *Synchronize* (line 158 — permission names), *Smart folders* (line 50), *Published* (line 94), *Unpublished* (line 97), *Forms* application (line 201), *Staging*, *Production* (line 125).
No improper all-caps usage. Page does not use all-caps for emphasis.
Punctuation is not formatted with bold/italic after formatted text throughout.

All headings use sentence case: 'Supported content', 'What is not synchronized?', 'Editing process when using content sync', 'Synchronize content', 'Pages', 'Sync moved or reordered pages', 'Choose the restoration time for synchronized content', 'Track incoming synchronizations'.
List items consistently use sentence case.
Single spaces between sentences and words. 4-space indentation used.
Serial comma used consistently (e.g., line 106: 'submission data, autoresponder settings, automation processes, form submission notifications, and the visibility configuration').
All 'for example' and 'e.g.' instances followed by commas (e.g., line 19: 'For example,'; line 117: 'For example,'; line 125: 'For example,').
Hyphens used without spaces (e.g., 'public-facing', 'page-id' in Liquid tags).
No em dashes found anywhere on the page.
Straight double quotes used throughout (e.g., line 17: 'instance', line 100: 'select').

Lists within markdown body stay within 3 levels. Lines 49, 66-67, 80-82 appear deeply indented but are inside {% table %}/{% cell %} Liquid blocks — the indentation is structural Liquid nesting, not list depth. Actual list content within cells is ≤2 levels deep.
No ellipsis characters found. 'etc.' not present either (not needed).
Full-page scan: zero Unicode en dashes (U+2013) or em dashes (U+2014) found. All dashes use '--' (lines 185, 220, 221). ANTI-002 (dash confusion) mitigation confirmed effective.
Page follows task-oriented approach — documents what users can accomplish (synchronize content items, pages, forms) rather than listing every UI element.
Title 'Content sync' uses sentence case.
Title 'Content sync' is a noun phrase for a feature/concept page.
No 'How to' prefix in page title.

Actionable headings use imperative verb phrases: 'Synchronize content', 'Sync moved or reordered pages', 'Sync forms with pages', 'Sync forms individually', 'Choose the restoration time for synchronized content', 'Track incoming synchronizations'.
Ordered lists used for step-by-step instructions (lines 143-152, 160-171, 207-212). Bullet points used for non-sequential information (supported content, limitations).
Each step contains one primary action (e.g., 'Open the Content hub application', 'Select the content items', 'Use the Sync action').
Steps use imperative mood: 'Open', 'Select', 'Use', 'Review', 'Choose'.
Modified line 158 is concise and focused. Adds necessary permission information without verbosity.
Line 158 leads with capability ('You can synchronize individual pages') then states the requirement ('you need Read and Synchronize page permissions').
No instances of 'please' found anywhere on the page.

Modified line 158 uses active voice ('you need', 'you can synchronize'). Pre-existing passive constructions (e.g., 'is synchronized' on lines describing system behavior) are acceptable per the invariant's exception clauses (avoiding condescension/awkwardness).
Present simple tense used throughout. Modified line: 'you need' (present simple).
American English used (e.g., 'synchronize' not 'synchronise').
Consistent terminology: 'synchronize/synchronization' used throughout (not mixed with 'sync' in prose — 'sync' only in UI action names and page title, matching actual UI).
Modified line does not use the pronoun 'it'. Pre-existing usage is minimal and contextually clear.
Indicative and imperative mood used. No subjunctive mood found.
Common contractions used where appropriate (e.g., line 93: 'can't' — actually 'cannot' is used; 'don't' used on line 66). Pre-existing content; not affected by change.
Input-agnostic verbs used: 'Select' (lines 129, 146, 152, 171, 211), 'Open' (lines 143, 160, 207), 'Enter' is not needed.

Modified line 158: 'you need *Read* and *Synchronize* page permissions' — uses 'you need' correctly. Pre-existing line 121 ('must be done by developers') has 'must' applied to 'changes' (subject), not directed at people — acceptable per invariant ('Must is acceptable when not writing about people').
Modified line leads with condition: 'To synchronize pages, you need...' — condition first, then instruction.
No inline code formatting issues. Page uses italics for permission names (*Read*, *Synchronize*) which is appropriate for named values, not code elements.
Callout types used appropriately: {% note %} for important prerequisites/impacts, {% info %} for additional recommendations. Severity order respected.

All callout boxes include bold titles: **Prerequisites** (line 24), **Recommended content size per synchronization** (line 134), **Duration of page content sync** (line 176), **Impact of synchronizing forms** (line 190), **Scheduling multiple synchronizations** (line 226), **Synchronization cleanup** (line 242).
No callout type names ('Note', 'Info', 'Warning') appear inside their corresponding callout boxes.
Callout types match content severity: {% note %} for prerequisites/important info, {% info %} for recommendations/additional context.
Table on lines 39-86 uses Liquid table syntax ({% table %}, {% row %}, {% cell %}).
Page starts with YAML front matter between --- delimiters (lines 1-15).
All required front matter fields present: title (Content sync), identifier (content_sync_xp), order (450), redirect_from (x/content_sync_xp), persona (business), license (2).
Identifier 'content_sync_xp' uses snake_case with _xp suffix for _documentation collection.
redirect_from includes 'x/content_sync_xp' matching the identifier.
License field set to 2 (advanced tier).
Admonition tags use correct Liquid syntax with matching end tags: {% note %}...{% endnote %} (lines 23/27, 175/179, 189/193, 225/231), {% info %}...{% endinfo %} (lines 133/139, 241/245).
Images use Liquid syntax: {% image content_sync_topology.png title=... %} (line 21), {% image sync_content_items.png title=... %} (line 147), etc.
UI/screenshot images include border=true: lines 147, 164, 199, 209, 222, 237. Topology diagram (line 21) correctly omits border.
Front matter includes related_pages: ['content_items_xp', 'JwKQC', 'content_sync_configuration_xp'].
Front matter toc configured with minHeadingLevel: 1, maxHeadingLevel: 3.
Icon tags use correct syntax: {% icon xp-arrow-leave-square color="rgb(21, 21, 21)" %} (lines 146, 164, 208), {% icon xp-three-dots-vertical %} (line 163), {% icon xp-bin color="red" %} (line 239).
In-page links use correct syntax: {% inpage_link "Choose the restoration time for synchronized content" linkText=... %} (lines 148, 165), {% inpage_link "Forms" linkText=... %} (line 67), etc.

All page_link tags reference _documentation collection pages. Collection attribute correctly omitted (defaults to documentation). Modified line 158: {% page_link permissions_pagelevel_xp anchor="Page permissions for synchronizing pages" linkText="page permissions" %} — correct syntax.

Modified line 158: anchor 'Page permissions for synchronizing pages' corresponds to the heading added to the target page by task T-001 (dependency). Writer-output confirms this anchor was updated to match the new heading.
Link text throughout is descriptive. Modified line 158: linkText='page permissions' accurately describes the target content. No vague 'click here' or 'this page' patterns found.
No 'Next steps' section added. Page structure unchanged from prior approved version.
Page is self-contained with rich cross-references to related topics. Modified line adds a useful cross-reference to the permissions page.
Page works for new and returning users. Prerequisites noted. Modified line adds helpful permission context without assuming prior knowledge.

Article structure intact: Title (Content sync), Introduction (lines 17-19), Body (supported content, editing process, synchronize content sections), Result (sync dialog shows progress — lines 154, 173, 214).
Introduction (lines 17-19) provides context about content synchronization, its purpose, and multi-environment workflow.
Task T-008 adds a cross-reference from the content sync page to the centralized permissions page, helping unify scattered permission information.

inapplicableInvariants.reason: No code samples or code blocks on this business-user how-to page.
No {% code %} or {% code_link %} blocks on this page.
Page does not use file downloads, videos, columns, panels, cards, banners, status badges, or arcade demos.
Invariant topics not present on this page (sign-in terminology, specific UI elements like checkboxes, code pluralization, path formatting, temperature, gender pronouns, etc.).
Not applicable to this update-crossrefs action on a business-user page.
Pre-existing front matter values not modified in this task. Previously validated.

additionalFindings.evidence: Headings follow correct hierarchy: h2 → h3 → h4 throughout. No skipped levels. 13 headings verified (h2: 4, h3: 5, h4: 3). h4 headings ('Sync moved or reordered pages', 'Sync forms with pages', 'Sync forms individually') each appear under h3 parents.

All Liquid tags have matching open/close pairs. {% table %}...{% endtable %}, {% note %}...{% endnote %}, {% info %}...{% endinfo %} all properly closed. No broken includes or invalid {% %} / {{ }} usage.

All cross-references use {% page_link %} or {% inpage_link %} Liquid syntax. No bare markdown links or broken references. Modified cross-reference on line 158 follows correct format with anchor and linkText attributes.
Lists, callouts, images, and icon tags follow the same patterns throughout. Bold for UI elements, italics for values/names, consistent icon syntax with color attributes.

ANTI-002 (dash confusion): Verified — all dashes on page use '--' (lines 185, 220, 221). Zero Unicode en dashes or em dashes. The attempt-2 writer correctly avoided the dash variant that caused rejection in prior runs.

STYLE-004 (page_link identifier verification): The modified cross-reference uses identifier 'permissions_pagelevel_xp' which matches the target page's actual front matter. Anchor updated to 'Page permissions for synchronizing pages' per T-001 dependency.
